title: Nonpareil combed marbling pattern
sources: Josef Halfer, The Progress of the Marbling Art (1893, public domain English translation 1894) — the combed patterns including nonpareil
notes: The classic nonpareil pattern: stone (drops laid), wide rake through one direction (produces wide combed stripes), fine rake through at right angles (produces the small repeated zigzag nonpareil texture). The two-comb step transforms straight bands into the characteristic tight ripple. Most recognisable historical marbling pattern; found on Victorian endpapers and legal documents.
